codeReview.summary: The refactor mostly centralizes UI lookups successfully, but it leaves prompt_engine and build_location_time_data still constructing ad-hoc lookup maps locally, so the feature does not fully satisfy its stated scope and architecture goal.

issues.description: The feature explicitly required replacing the two ad-hoc ts_map constructions in prompt_engine.py with CacheManager lookups, but this commit only wrapped the same local dict comprehension in _build_ts_by_id() and still builds a fresh map when callers omit ts_by_id. Current production callers in src/services/deduction.py still omit the new argument, so prompt building continues rebuilding per-call maps instead of using centralized indexes.

build_location_time_data() still constructs a local char_by_id dict instead of using centralized CacheManager/AppState indexes. The feature description explicitly called out that build_location_time_data should work with centralized indexes, so this leaves one of the targeted ad-hoc lookup constructions in place and the refactor incomplete.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker skill mandates a TDD red phase, but the worker correctly skipped it for this pure refactor and documented that the step was not applicable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff for ui-index-migration includes skillFeedback.suggestedChanges noting that the TDD red phase was not applicable for a pure refactoring with no behavior change.

summary: Review failed: the commit centralizes several UI lookups, but it does not fully complete the migration because prompt_engine still rebuilds its own time-slot lookup maps on the live call paths and build_location_time_data still builds a local character map.
